sessions.title: Registration/Conference Check-In
sessions.description: Be sure to register your attendance and check-in with us - and don't miss out on grabbing your badge and swag!

sessions.title: New Administrator Welcome (NAW) *pre-registered attendees only*
sessions.description: A social session geared towards REDCap administrators attending REDCapCon for the first time. The purpose of this gathering will be to welcome new attendees and help them get acquainted with others before the conference starts. Like an orientation, but more fun!  *Session registration required.
sessions.speaker: Various

sessions.title: Welcome to OKC!
sessions.description: Feel free to use this time and space to hang out and chat with REDCap colleagues new and old as you prepare to embark on your epic REDCapCon journey!

sessions.title: Installing a Local REDCap Instance
sessions.description: Learn how to install a local REDCap instance! This is also useful if you will be attending any coding or EM sessions later on as well.   Note: If you're using a work computer, you will want to have admin rights, including access to terminal or the command line depending on what OS you're using.
sessions.speaker: Kyle McGuffin

sessions.title: Welcome Dinner & MVP Award Announcement
sessions.description: Don't miss our dinner gathering-good eats, great connections, and the big reveal of this year's MVP!

sessions.title: Breakfast
Late Conference Check-In
sessions.description: If you weren't able to check-in/ register your attendance on Sunday, now's the time to grab your badge and some swag!

sessions.title: Opening Remarks
Keynote
Plenary Session with Paul Harris
15-Minute Break
All-Star Awards
Scaling Public Health Programs with REDCap: A Naloxone Distribution System Serving 1,000+ Programs
sessions.description: In 2023, the Washington State Department of Health migrated its statewide overdose education and naloxone distribution program from spreadsheets to a network of connected REDCap projects that manage onboarding, ordering, fulfillment, invoicing, and reporting across multiple funding streams. Since then, the same staff have scaled the program to support more than 1,000 organizations across all 39 counties and distribute over 750,000 naloxone kits; this session highlights the system’s design, lessons learned, and evolution as demand and funding complexity increased.
sessions.speaker: Noah Frank

sessions.title: REDCap Software: What's New and What's Next
sessions.description: Learn about new and upcoming features in REDCap
sessions.speaker: Rob Taylor

sessions.title: New(er) Admin Panel
sessions.description: Join us in this panel discussion with new(er) admins and their thoughts on REDCap and the consortum!
sessions.speaker: Various

sessions.title: Creative Uses of REDCap I (Beginner level)
sessions.description: Discover some creative uses of REDCap strictly using basic REDCap functionality: no external modules, no hooks, no plugins, etc.
sessions.speaker: Ashleigh Lewis

sessions.title: Lunch
API biostats session
sessions.speaker: Daniel Maya, Will Beasley

sessions.title: External Modules Showcase!
sessions.description: Join us for the third annual showcase celebrating the creativity and ingenuity of REDCap developers! In this session, community leaders will present their standout external modules, demonstrating how these powerful tools are solving real-world challenges and enhancing REDCap functionality in exciting ways.
sessions.speaker: Various

sessions.title: REDCap: Unique Problems and How to Fix Them
sessions.speaker: Katie Hofmann, Leila Deering

sessions.title: 15-Minute Break
Beyond the Build: Documentation Strategies for REDCap Administration and Project Success
sessions.speaker: Chris Sorenson, Bas de Veer

sessions.title: REDCap Security - Administrative and Technical Practices
sessions.speaker: Chloe Wildman, Greg Neils

sessions.title: Transforming Local Research Via MyCap: Empowering Administrator Knowledge & Growing Local Innovation
sessions.description: As research expands to multisite, hybrid, decentralized, and remote models, the MyCap Mobile App helps investigators engage participants wherever they are. This session introduces MyCap for REDCap administrators, covering how it enhances participant engagement, supports return of results, key use cases, implementation considerations, and ways it extends REDCap beyond traditional surveys.
sessions.speaker: Emily Serdoz

sessions.title: 15-Minute Break
sessions.description: A short break where you can leisurely head over to the poster and committee session!

sessions.title: Poster & Committee Session
sessions.description: Take a stroll through the posters to discover the incredible ways our administrators are leveraging REDCap at their institutions (and beyond)! Each poster highlights unique projects, creative solutions, and impactful outcomes that showcase the power of REDCap in action.  Don't forget to visit the committee tables too! Learn about the important work our committees are doing to advance REDCap initiatives and find out how you can contribute to our collaborative community.
sessions.speaker: Various

sessions.title: Optional Early Bird Session
Late Conference Check-In
sessions.description: If you weren't able to check-in/ register your attendance before, now's the time to grab your badge and some swag!

sessions.title: Breakfast
Opening Remarks and Additional All-Star Awards
Mobile Apps: What's New & What's Next
sessions.description: Learn about MyCap's latest features and enhancements, and what's coming in the future.
sessions.speaker: Emily Serdoz, Byungsuk Choi

sessions.title: The Connective Tissue: REDCap Infrastructure for Harm Reduction, Outreach, & Public Health Ops
sessions.description: Skid Row is a major epicenter of the intersecting crises of homelessness and substance use. This session demonstrates how Homeless Health Care Los Angeles uses REDCap as a centralized platform to support community-based public health services, creating scalable, automated workflows for paraprofessional staff with limited computer literacy. Through live examples, we will showcase technical strategies for client and program monitoring across five sites, capturing critical low-threshold service data often missed in fragmented care environments.
sessions.speaker: Lauren Shapiro

sessions.title: Clinical Data: What's New & What's Next
sessions.description: Learn about what's new and our plans for the future with clinical data features that have been integrated into REDCap.
sessions.speaker: Alex Cheng, Katelyn Benhoff

sessions.title: 15-Minute Break
Creative Uses of REDCap II (Intermediate-Advanced level)
sessions.description: Learn some intermediate to advanced creative uses in REDCap using basic REDCap functionality (and maybe a dash extra).
sessions.speaker: Ashleigh Lewis

sessions.title: GAIN: Delivering Synchronous and Asynchronous Autism Training with REDCap
sessions.description: GAIN (Global Autism Interactive Network) is a researcher-led professional development program that uses REDCap to deliver Autisim Spectrum Disorders (ASD) assessment training, including ADOS-2 instruction, webinars, trainee scoring, CE tracking, and self-service account management. Using only standard REDCap functionality and a custom Survey Queue, the program supports both live and asynchronous learning through Zoom links and embedded videos, with the goal of improving clinical diagnosis of ASD to a global audience.
sessions.speaker: Lisa Hallberg

sessions.title: REDCap AI: What's New & What's Next
sessions.description: Learn about REDCap's latest AI features and enhancements, and what's coming in the future.
sessions.speaker: Cathy Shyr

sessions.title: 10-Minute Break
Validated, Launched … Decommissioned: A REDCap Case Study in Hard Lessons
sessions.description: This session presents a real-world case study of building, validating, and ultimately decommissioning a validated REDCap environment. We will explore validation strategies, compliance requirements, governance, staffing, and ongoing maintenance, as well as the institutional factors that led to shutdown. Attendees will gain practical insights into the sustainability, organizational alignment, and long-term costs associated with validated REDCap implementations.
sessions.speaker: Heather Lansky

sessions.title: REDCap at Scale: Lessons from Outbreak Response and Emergency Preparedness
sessions.description: This presentation highlights how REDCap supports adaptable emergency preparedness and response workflows, including intake, monitoring, routing, and tracking during public health emergencies. Using real-world outbreak response examples, we will examine scalable design patterns, solutions to challenges such as high-volume intake and cross-project automation, and opportunities to expand REDCap’s capabilities for large-scale emergency operations.
sessions.speaker: Andrew Delicata

sessions.title: Group Photo
Lunch
AI assisted project design and implementation
sessions.speaker: Erik Risinger, Gunther Rezniczek, Mamata Tokala

sessions.title: Are we really keeping all these REDCap Projects?
sessions.description: This interactive session explores how institutions manage REDCap project retention, archival, and deletion decisions while meeting record retention requirements and balancing resource constraints. Through this discussion, we will share approaches to handling completed projects, defining responsibilities, and determining what data must be preserved versus retired.
sessions.speaker: Lynn Simpson, Scott Carey, Jeff Hawley, Daniel Carey, Thomas Wilson

sessions.title: Data Management in REDCap: Suggested Best Practices
sessions.description: This session will highlight various suggested best practices for data management in REDCap. Time will be included for audience members to discuss the suggested practices and to offer their own.
sessions.speaker: Terri Scott

sessions.title: External Module Cooking and Baking
sessions.description:
Programming External Modules and variety of coding techniques, such as, use of CRON features, API handling, CSV data handling, Data handling conversions and more.
How to prep and cook code in one pan.
Design, Principles and guidance of fundamental recipes with on hand ingredients.
sessions.speaker: David Heskett, Kyle McGuffin

sessions.title: How to Build REDCap with Excel
sessions.description: Data Dictionaries are just .csv's - so why not leverage all of Microsoft Excel's functionality to build them? This session will look at ways you can use Excel functions to generate syntactically correct calculated fields and branching logic for use in REDCap projects. Examples will include building calculated fields for lookup tables, soft warnings at the end of a survey if a question is left blank, and how to create a mapping template to map REDCap variables to NDA definitions.
sessions.speaker: Ethan Goldstein

sessions.title: 15-Minute Break
Exploring the REDCap SQL Back End Tables
sessions.description: In this session you will be introduced to the MySQL database tables that underpin all of REDCap. We will explore the REDCap project table, and the data and log tables. Expect lots of examples. SQL knowledge assumed. Note: we will not be discussing dynamic SQL fields - that will be a later session! ()
sessions.speaker: Paul Litwin, Lisa Hallberg

sessions.title: From Complicated to Common: Advancing Uptake & Usability of NIH Common Data Elements  through REDCap
sessions.description: This session will explore challenges and opportunities around CDE use and what is important to the NIH vs researchers for CDEs.  We'll look at the RAG model and how we've looked at CDE use here at Vanderbilt Health and what we're doing with REDCap to help make it easier to find and use the correct CDE.
sessions.speaker: Steph Duda, Colleen Lawrence, Lina Sulieman

sessions.title: From Story to System: Building a Sustainable REDCap Program Using REDCap
sessions.description: This session focuses on practical design patterns for implementing REDCap-based administrative systems. We demonstrate how documentation, decision tracking, and workflow governance can be structured directly within REDCap to transform fragmented administrative work into reusable, data-driven systems. Attendees will leave with actionable design patterns and starting points for building scalable administrative infrastructure within their own REDCap environments.
sessions.speaker: Jason Lones

sessions.title: REDCap Rewards - supporting participant compensation via REDCap + Tango integration
sessions.speaker: Michelle Jones, Francesco Delacqua

sessions.title: eSignatures in REDCap
sessions.speaker: Theresa Baker, Gunther Rezniczek

sessions.title: 15-Minute Break
AI: Risk-Based Evaluation and Governance Framework
sessions.description: This session explores a risk-based framework for evaluating, governing, and deploying AI-enabled solutions within REDCap. Attendees will learn practical approaches to balancing innovation, compliance, data quality, and oversight while supporting the responsible use of AI in research and operational environments.
sessions.speaker: Paul Harris, Cathy Shyr

sessions.title: API 2.0
sessions.description: API 2.0
sessions.speaker: Gunther Rezniczek

sessions.title: Inside the RVP Validation Content Management System (VCMS)
sessions.description: This session showcases the REDCap-based Validation Content Management System (VCMS), developed to streamline validation activities including requirements management, testing, traceability, evidence collection, and change control. Attendees will learn how this RSVC-aligned approach supports validation, compliance, audit readiness, and ongoing revalidation efforts for organizations using Rapid Validation Process resources.
sessions.speaker: Theresa Baker

sessions.title: Project Migration Panel: strategies and lessons learned
sessions.description: Project Migration Panel: strategies and lessons learned
sessions.speaker: Amanda Miller, Greg Neils, Muhammad Asim Mubeen, Jenn Mauck, Savvy DeHart

sessions.title: The Engine Behind the Research: Connecting Cores Through REDCap Innovation
sessions.description: This presentation will describe the Vanderbilt Memory and Alzheimer’s Center (VMAC) data infrastructure, highlighting a scalable, multi-core ecosystem built around REDCap as its central platform. As part of the Alzheimer’s Disease Research Center (ADRC), VMAC supports multiple functional cores with distinct operational needs, requiring a flexible yet integrated data architecture. By leveraging REDCap in combination with internal governance structures and external research tools, VMAC has developed a unified system that promotes efficiency, data integrity, and cross-core collaboration.
sessions.speaker: Daniel Ibarra-Scurr

sessions.title: 15-Minute Break
Collaborative Spaces: "Birds of a Feather" Small Groups and Workshops
sessions.description: Looking to connect with others who share your interests? Join us for our "Birds of a Feather" session-an open, participant-driven space where you can either start your own meet-up or join an existing one!  Whether you're diving into a specific REDCap topic or just want to network with like-minded peers, this is your chance to create meaningful conversations. Follow the link below to propose a session or browse the list of current meet-ups and find one that sparks your interest!  https://redcap.link/birdsofafeather26
sessions.speaker: See Link in Description

sessions.title: Annual Fun Run/Walk
sessions.description:
For those wanting to get fresh morning air and some steps in! 
Meet across from the Omni Hotel entrance, by the statue of the white feather. Check out our dashboard page for more information!  https://redcap.vumc.org/redcap_v17.3.1/index.php?route=ProjectDashController:view&dash_id=5535&pid=224495

sessions.title: Optional Early Bird Session
Breakfast
Late Conference Check-In
sessions.description: If you weren't able to check-in/ register your attendance before, now's the time to grab your badge and some swag!

sessions.title: An Interactive Framework for Streamlined Requirements Gathering & Project Delivery
sessions.description: We will present an interactive intake framework designed to go beyond collecting the standard requirements needed to build user-centered projects by capturing the complexities and nuances of research workflows that often go unaddressed in static intake forms. We’ve created a centralized hub for collecting all study, project, and user information to help establish clear expectations. Built-in automations reduce manual effort by programmatically generating cost ranges and timeline estimates, while integration with Jira enables automatic creation, updating, and tracking of project tasks. Researchers gain access to a dashboard that follows projects from intake to delivery, providing transparency throughout the build process.
sessions.speaker: Laura Sullivan

sessions.title: Automating Fraud Analysis for Research Survey Data
sessions.description: Survey research increasingly faces threats from fraudulent responses including bots, speeders, and pattern-based fraud that compromise data integrity. While front-end prevention methods provide initial barriers, systematic post-collection analysis remains time-intensive and requires statistical expertise many researchers lack. This presentation demonstrates the development and pilot implementation of an algorithm-powered fraud detection tool built with Python that allows researchers to upload survey data and receive fraud risk scores for each response, streamlining data quality assessment for REDCap surveys.
sessions.speaker: Viktoriya Babicheva

sessions.title: Dynamic SQL in REDCap: Introduction and Beyond!
sessions.description: This hands-on workshop is a fusion of two SQUIRL trainings: Dynamic SQL: Introduction, and Dynamic SQL: Beyond the Basics, developed by Paul Litwin and Lisa Hallberg. It was presented last year at REDCapCon 2025, and will teach attendees how to use the Dynamic SQL field in REDCap projects to query data across projects.
sessions.speaker: Lisa Hallberg, Paul Litwin

sessions.title: Empowering REDCap Users: A Scalable, On-Demand Approach to Training
sessions.description: This presentation highlights the development of a fully on-demand REDCap training program, from identifying user needs and implementing a learning management system to delivering accessible, scenario-based training at scale. Attendees will learn strategies for creating effective online REDCap education that supports both new and advanced users while improving efficiency and institutional alignment.
sessions.speaker: Ann Gleason, Courtney Howell

sessions.title: PROMIS in your pocket: Remote assessment with Mobile Toolbox and MyCap
sessions.description: The Mobile Toolbox (MTB) is a growing library of brief, validated, and sensitive measures delivered through REDCap’s free participant-facing MyCap mobile application, enabling low-burden assessment of health and functioning on participants’ own smartphones in English and Spanish. This presentation introduces the MTB and its new PROMIS integration in MyCap, highlighting its potential to amplify patient voices in research and clinical decision-making through scalable remote assessment. Attendees will learn how to leverage these measures on their projects, the advantages of the new assessment framework, as well as setup procedures, practical considerations, and emerging use cases.
sessions.speaker: Elizabeth Dworak

sessions.title: REDCap Cypress Testing Framework
sessions.speaker: Kyle McGuffin

sessions.title: 15-Minute Break
ARROW: AI-Assisted, Audit-Ready Validation of REDCap Study Databases
sessions.description: In regulated clinical research, study databases must be systematically tested, and the evidence of database validation documented before use, yet this process is often still performed manually through lengthy field-by-field testing and screen recording. ARROW is an AI-assisted, open-source tool that reads the study protocol, REDCap data dictionary and automatically generates, executes, and video-records functional validation tests across study CRFs. It produces an audit-ready evidence package with per-instrument recordings and structured pass/fail reporting, supporting reproducible and regulation-aligned validation of REDCap study databases.
sessions.speaker: Yama Farooq Mujadidi

sessions.title: Building Powerful External Modules: Lessons from Harmonist Hub
sessions.description: This session showcases the range of experiences you can deliver with External Modules, using Harmonist Hub as a concrete example of what’s possible. Rather than a build tutorial, we’ll highlight advanced capabilities and design patterns that enable rich, application-like workflows—such as launching REDCap surveys in pop-up/iframe windows and embedding purpose-built utilities directly into a module. We’ll also demo Hub Updates, an in-hub link that supports maintaining data dictionaries in an intuitive, user-friendly way.
sessions.speaker: Eva Bascompte Moragas

sessions.title: Epic to REDCap Integration: Best Practices
sessions.description: A practical guide to implementing the Epic to REDCap integration using a FHIR server. Best practices, recommendations, and where we struggled will be discussed.
sessions.speaker: Juan Artigas, Stephen Stremmel

sessions.title: Novel uses for REDCap in QI work
sessions.description: Have you ever wondered how to use REDCap to manage your QI portfolios? This session will show you how to use REDCap with frontline staff to track quality work such as hospital acquired conditions (HACs), apparent cause analyses (ACAs), monthly quality audits (QIC, hand hygiene), and more. We will look at the builds themselves along output displays in Power BI, REDCap dashboards and REDCap reports. The builds include, but aren't limited to, use of surveys, alerts/notifications, automatic survey invitations, and action tags.
sessions.speaker: Rachel Wenthe

sessions.title: Preventing Fraudulent Activity in REDCap Surveys
sessions.speaker: Esmot Ara Begum, Scott Carey

sessions.title: REDCap Supporting REDCap: An Internal Ticketing System for a Small Program
sessions.description: This session builds on previous presentations demonstrating how to develop an internal Ticketing System using REDCap. Designed for a very small program (a team of one), it describes the decision-making process that led to using REDCap to manage REDCap support, along with the creative uses employed. The presentation also discusses lessons learned and anticipated strategies for scaling the system as the REDCap program expands.
sessions.speaker: Page Van Meter

sessions.title: 15-Minute Break
Digital Accessibility and REDCap
sessions.description: A presentation of accessibility considerations and compliance as it relates to REDCap surveys and institutional use.
sessions.speaker: Page Van Meter, Courtney Howell

sessions.title: Me, Myself & I: REDCap Administration Solo
sessions.description: A discussion section of administrators that are solo (or have very small teams) about how we find resources, unique challenges we face, and how to support each other.
sessions.speaker: Madison Albright

sessions.title: Other/Misc Discussion
REDCap Meets AI: Building Intelligent Reporting Pipelines with Claude
sessions.description: This session will demonstrate how to integrate the REDCap API with advanced AI assistants, specifically Anthropic's Claude, to streamline and automate scientific data reporting. We will explore how to securely extract clinical registry or study data via the API and utilize Claude’s reasoning capabilities to draft data summaries, identify trends, and format complex reports. Attendees will learn practical workflows for setting up these automated pipelines while maintaining rigorous data governance and quality control. Finally, we will discuss real-world use cases, potential pitfalls, and the future of AI-driven research administration.
sessions.speaker: Greg Keane

sessions.title: REDCap New Administrator Playbook: What Breaks and How to Fix It
sessions.description: This session prepares new REDCap administrators to recognize and resolve common issues in project setup, user access, and system behavior. It emphasizes practical troubleshooting using REDCap tools like logging and reports. Attendees will learn how to identify root causes and determine when escalation is needed.
sessions.speaker: Scott Robertson

sessions.title: REDCap SHARE Use Case Brainstorming and Q&A
sessions.description: Getting participant EHR data from external institutions is typically complicated - release forms, data sharing agreements, rolls of red tape. REDCap SHARE simplifies this process by harnessing the power of the Cures Act Final Rule and REDCap's ability to connect to any US certified EHR system. Participants mediate the process and send their EHR data directly to your REDCap instance for action and analysis. Join us to discuss potential SHARE use cases at your institution and to have your questions answered. *SHARE is available as a part of REDCap+.
sessions.speaker: Katelyn Benhoff, Francesco Delacqua, Alex Cheng, Adam Lewis

sessions.title: 15-Minute Break
Confessions of an AI-Assisted REDCap Developer: Building Better REDCap So
sessions.description: What if your next REDCap project could be completed in half the time? This session explores how AI is transforming the way developers build External Modules, solve complex technical problems, automate infrastructure, and create documentation. Using real examples from varioius REDCap environments, attendees will learn practical techniques for turning AI into a trusted engineering partner while avoiding common pitfalls.
sessions.speaker: Greg Neils

sessions.title: Managing Twilio at Scale: Tips, Tricks, and Lessons Learned from a Centralized Management Model
sessions.description: A2P 10DLC registration has become a recurring pain point for REDCap administrators and users. In this session, we will share how we addressed that challenge by moving to a centralized Twilio account model supported by a REDCap integration for account and user management. We will discuss lessons learned from restructuring our A2P 10DLC workflows, along with practical tips and templates for successful campaign registration and ongoing maintenance. Attendees will leave with concrete strategies they can adapt to simplify Twilio management at their own institutions.
sessions.speaker: Kiley Yeaman

sessions.title: Our Favorite EM's!!
sessions.description: A round-robin presentation by 5 Sr. REDCap Administrators highlighting their favorite / most-valuable EM's. With opportunity for group discussion at the end.
sessions.speaker: Scott Carey, Jeff Hawley, Lindsay Berrigan, Greg Neils, Leila Deering

sessions.title: REDCap CDIS-based Healthcare Provider Organization Participation in All of Us
sessions.description: The All of Us program is piloting an HPO-Lite model of participation. Instead of setting up infrastructure to extract EHR data from participants from the backened, institutions would be able to use REDCap CDIS to collect and contribute data with FHIR to the program. Come discuss what participating in All of Us through HPO-Lite might look like at your institution.
sessions.speaker: Alex Cheng, Lina Sulieman

sessions.title: REDCap Learning Lab
sessions.description: Overview of the new REDCap Learning lab and more information for those interested in signing up.
sessions.speaker: Irma Mare, Amanda Miller

sessions.title: Web Dev 2.0: Spec-Driven Prototyping in REDCap
sessions.description:
Web Dev 101 (REDCapCon 2023) mapped the building blocks of web development in
REDCap; Rapid Prototyping in REDCap (REDCapCon 2025) introduced the Conflux
workflow for developing them like a real codebase. Web Dev 2.0 puts the two
together and adds spec-driven development to prototype an interactive
study-team dashboard. AI use here is a dial, not a commitment. Use it up front
to build your spec templates, or hand it the spec and let it run start to
finish; both paths start from the same specs. Attendees leave with the full
repo: spec templates for each stage, reusable context files, and the
prototype.
sessions.speaker: Jess Hale

sessions.title: Lunch
Door Prizes!
sessions.speaker: Brenda Minor

sessions.title: Eleven Years of Invasive Bacterial Disease Surveillance in Nepal
sessions.description: High-quality surveillance data are essential for monitoring infectious disease trends and informing public health action, but sustaining data completeness & consistency over many years remains difficult, particularly in resource-limited settings. This talk presents eleven years of invasive bacterial disease surveillance from 2014 to 2025 in Nepal, focusing on how daily data review, structured query resolution, staff feedback, & system evolution strengthened data quality. The data management system evolved from Microsoft Access to REDCap, supporting real-time data capture, automated validation checks, & improved integration of clinical, laboratory, & outcome data. The talk will share practical lessons on transforming routine surveillance workflows into more reliable, adaptive, and decision-ready data systems.
sessions.speaker: Yama Farooq Mujadidi

sessions.title: MyCap-Based Behavioral Intervention Promoting Physical Activity & Mindfulness to People with Cancer
sessions.description: People living with breast and gynecologic cancers commonly experience severe fatigue and reduced quality of life. Physical activity and mindfulness practices can improve fatigue and physical functioning in these populations. We tested the feasibility and acceptability of a MyCap-delivered behavioral intervention that included didactic lessons, experiential exercises, and gamification elements designed to promote physical activity and mindfulness practices. Results supported the intervention’s feasibility, acceptability, and preliminary efficacy.
sessions.speaker: Michael Robertson

sessions.title: The Beginning to Now with Bas
sessions.speaker: Bas de Veer

sessions.title: 15-Minute Break
The Yale REDCap User Group: A Community Exchange of Ideas and Assistance
sessions.description: Beginning in Summer 2025, Yale University launched the Yale REDCap User Group, and invited all people involved in building REDCap databases across the institution to participate. Over the past year, this has led to a community of nearly 80 people meeting monthly (originally biweekly) to discuss REDCap, and a Teams channel where people can post questions to get crowd-sourced responses from other project designers. Our meeting presentations have featured both Yale staff members as well as guests from other institutions as part of an "exchange" system - give a guest presentation at Yale, and we'll give a guest presentation at your institution. This has also fostered collaborations that wouldn't have otherwise been possible, such as connecting a SQL developer with REDCap experience to a user who had the need for SQL fields in her database.
sessions.speaker: Ethan Goldstein

sessions.title: The Personal Effect of being a REDCap Admin
sessions.description: As REDCap admins we sometimes don't realize effect that our support has on individuals. In this session, we will break down the numbers to explore how personal it is to be a REDCap admin.
sessions.speaker: Thomas Wilson

sessions.title: Competition Winners/Awards and 2026 Host Thank You
sessions.speaker: Vanderbilt Health Team

sessions.title: 2027 REDCapCon Host Announcement
sessions.speaker: (it's a surprise!)

sessions.title: Closing remarks and event dismissal
sessions.speaker: Paul Harris
